revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:市野々王子]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nachikatsuura|qid=Q1346730|1=Nachikatsuura}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.66294444444444|N|135.9117222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}|part_of={{ill|Kujūku-ōji|qid=Q3200280|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines}}}}
'''Ichinono Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== '''Ichinono Ōji''' (市野々王子) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nachikatsuura|en|Nachikatsuura|qid=Q1346730|lt=Nachikatsuura|1=Nachikatsuura}}, {{ill|Higashimuro District, Wakayama|en|Higashimuro District, Wakayama|qid=Q1131175|lt=Higashimuro District|1=Higashimuro District, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, and its precincts are part of the national historic site "{{ill|Kumano Kodō|lt=Kumano Kodō|lt=Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野参詣道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野古道}}" (designated on November 2, 2000).
== History == The exact founding date of Ichinono Ōji is unclear, but it has long been a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kumano Nachi Taisha|en|Kumano Nachi Taisha|qid=Q710359|lt=-->{{ill}}, with maintenance costs covered by the Taisha. Records of {{ill|Kumano pilgrimage|ja|熊野詣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山|1=Kumano Three Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10403700}} from the medieval period mention the shrine, suggesting it was established no later than that time. In the diary of {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada|ja|藤原宗忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Munetada|1=Fujiwara no Munetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623159}} ("{{ill|Chuyuki|ja|中右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuyuki|1=Chūyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363231}}"), it is referred to as "Ichino," and in the pilgrimage record of {{ill|Empress Shomeimon-in|ja|修明門院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shomeimon-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原重子|1=Fujiwara no Shigeko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623607}}, it appears as "Ichinono."
In the {{ill|Nachi Pilgrimage Mandala|ja|那智参詣曼荼羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nachi Pilgrimage Mandala|1=Nachi Pilgrimage Mandala|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642742}}, a small shrine depicted just before the Futase Bridge is believed to be Ichinono Ōji. The {{ill|Kii Shokufudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shokufudoki|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604964}} suggests the shrine's name derives from a market ("ichi") held there for pilgrims. Historical documents from the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}} refer to it mainly as "Ichino," with "Ichinono Ōji" or "Wakame Ōji" becoming established names in the early modern period.
There are differing theories regarding the shrine's original location. Some believe it has always been at its current site, while others propose it was relocated from a place called {{ill|Bunmei no Oka|ja|文明の岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei no Oka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, about 100 meters north. Until the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, a {{ill|Konpira shrine|ja|金毘羅社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646488}} stood at Bunmei no Oka, which was merged with Ichinono Ōji in 1873 when it became independent from Nachi Taisha under the name Ōji Shrine.
== Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=Honden|1=Honden}} - Wooden nagare-zukuri with three shrines in one structure, copper-roofed. * Haiden and Shrine Office - Wooden, irimoya-zukuri with tile roofing. * Suzumon - Wooden gate with copper roofing. * One {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} - Stone Shinmei-style torii.
=== Auxiliary Shrines === * Jinushi Yatagarasu Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Taketomimi no Mikoto|ja|建角耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taketomimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Tradition holds that Ichinono is inhabited by descendants of {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|qid=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}.
=== Sugiyasha === 'Sugiyasha' (杉屋社) refers to a site north of Ichinono Ōji, believed to be its original location. It contains the remains of shrine foundation stones and the "{{ill|Yodou-ishi|ja|影向石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodou-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," a stone said to mark where {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} descended to earth.
=== Gokura === Located south of the shrine, these former rice storehouses for tax rice are town-designated historic sites (designated on March 1, 1970).
== Transportation == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|qid=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} - Get off at {{ill|Nachi Station|en|Nachi Station|qid=Q2584944|lt=Nachi Station|1=Nachi Station}}. * Take a {{ill|Kumano Kotsu|ja|熊野交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kotsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野御坊南海バス|1=Kumano Gobo Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568871}} bus to "Iseki" or "Ichinono Elementary School" stops, then walk south about 100 meters from the school along the old road within a residential area.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * "Wakayama Prefecture," edited by the "{{ill|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4093011}}" Editorial Committee, Kadokawa Shoten, 1985. * Ritsu Nishi, "Kumano Kodo Michishirube," Arao Seibundo, 1987. * Yasutaka Hasegawa, "Kumano Ōji Junpai Guidebook," Shinpū Shobo, 2007.
== See Also == * {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji}} * {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodo|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=8015 Ōji Shrine (Ichinono)] (Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office)
{{reflist}}
{{Ninety-Nine Ōji}}
{{translated page|ja|市野々王子|version=98045319|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:市野々王子]] [[zh:市野々王子]]

|{{ill|Inabane-ōji|fr|4=ja|5=稲葉根王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabane-ōji|qq=}} | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市・上富田町)#一ノ瀬王子|一ノ瀬王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市・上富田町)#鮎川王子|鮎川王子]] | style="background:#ffddaa" |{{ill|Takijiri-ōji|fr|4=ja|5=滝尻王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri-ōji|qq=}} | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#不寝王子|不寝王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[近露王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#比曽原王子|比曽原王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[継桜王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#中ノ河王子|中ノ河王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#小広王子|小広王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#熊瀬川王子|熊瀬川王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#岩神王子|岩神王子]] | Ōji|ja|発心門王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosshinmon Ōji|qq=}} | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)#水呑王子|水呑王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市本宮町)#伏拝王子|伏拝王子]] |
